messages.content: Build a pure Decider for an Ingredient aggregate in TypeScript. Export initialState, decide, and evolve with discriminated unions. Reject restocking an ingredient that has been discontinued.
Write an event-sourced Decider for a Recipe aggregate. Constraints: strict types, no any, exhaustive switch with a never default, typed DomainError, and reject publishing a recipe with no steps.
Explain briefly how a Decider shapes the lifecycle of a CookingSession aggregate, then show a working typed implementation with start, logStep, pause, resume, and finish commands.
Build a typed Decider for a FoodInspection aggregate (schedule, conduct, issueReport, close). Inspect pattern: reject closing before a report is issued.
What's the TypeScript way to implement a Decider for a NutritionProfile aggregate that tracks declared macros and rejects locking an empty profile? Show a minimal example.
Write a TypeScript Decider for a DietPlan aggregate. Constraints: discriminated Command/Event/State, typed DomainError, reject activating a plan with zero days, reject extending an expired plan.
Build a projection that folds MealSwap events into a per-user read model with totalSwaps and lastSwapAt. Use a pure project(view, event) function.
What's the event-sourcing way to derive a RecipeTest leaderboard view (top ratings per recipe) from a projection? Show a minimal TypeScript implementation.
Build a PantryStock read-model projection that aggregates per-ingredient available grams from inbound and outbound events. Pure project function, exhaustive switch.

Add a set-based allergen index to this AllergenTag projection so queries can answer which recipes contain a given allergen. Keep project pure and exhaustive.

```typescript
export type AllergenTagEvent = { type: "AllergenTagged"; recipeId: string; allergen: string } | { type: "AllergenRemoved"; recipeId: string; allergen: string };
```
Build a versioned Snapshot scheme for a Recipe aggregate. V1 has id/title, V2 adds cuisine, V3 adds servings. Provide upgraders and a loader that runs them in order.

Explain briefly why snapshots need a version tag when the aggregate shape evolves, then show a typed TypeScript Snapshot scheme for a CookingSession aggregate with two upgraders (v1 adds chefId, v2 adds durationMs).
Write a versioned Snapshot upgrade chain for DietPlan. Constraints: two upgraders (v1 adds calorieTarget, v2 adds macroSplit), CURRENT_VERSION constant, throw on unknown version.

Build a TypeScript write path for Ingredient events with optimistic concurrency: read the current stream revision, append with that expectedRevision, and throw a typed ConcurrencyConflict on mismatch.
How do I guard a FoodInspection append against lost updates using expectedRevision? Show a minimal TypeScript helper that turns the store's conflict signal into a domain-level error.
Wrap a CommandHandler for a Recipe aggregate in Effect.gen. Load the stream, decide, append with expectedRevision. Keep decide and evolve pure.
What does a thin CommandHandler for a CookingSession look like in TypeScript? Read the stream, fold into state, decide, then appendToStream with expectedRevision. Show a minimal example.

Write a TypeScript CommandHandler for a NutritionProfile aggregate. Constraints: thin orchestration (no business logic in the handler), typed EventStore interface, call the pure decide and evolve, append with expectedRevision.
Build an Effect.gen-wrapped CommandHandler for PantryStock. Load, decide, append. Use a local EventStore interface and a pure decide/evolve.
Build a FoodInspection checkpoint-driven subscription runner in TypeScript. Read last checkpoint, consume events, project into a read model, then write the new checkpoint after successful projection.
Explain briefly why the checkpoint must be written AFTER the projection has been applied, then show a typed TypeScript RecipeTest subscription runner that honours that rule.

Add resumable checkpointing to this AllergenTag projection runner so it continues from the last persisted position after a crash.

```typescript
// existing: projection runs from 0 every restart (wasteful and wrong)
```
Build a generic aggregateStream helper in TypeScript that folds an async iterable of events using a pure reducer. Apply it to an Ingredient when function.
How do I fold a stream of Recipe events back into aggregate state using a generic aggregateStream helper? Show a minimal TypeScript example with a pure when function.
Write a TypeScript aggregateStream helper and a DietPlan when reducer. Constraints: strict types, exhaustive switch, the helper accepts an AsyncIterable<E> and returns Promise<S>.

Build an upcasting function that migrates old Recipe event shapes to the current schema. Old v1 RecipeCreated has `name`; current uses `title`. Old v1 RecipeStepAdded has no fields; current adds `stepIndex`.

Explain briefly how upcasting lets you evolve event shape without rewriting history, then show a typed CookingSession upcast function for v1 -> v2 where v2 adds a chefId field defaulting from the session id.
Build a MealSwap read-model rebuild routine in TypeScript. Read events in batches from a revision cursor, project them, persist the batch, advance the cursor, support resume via a `from` option.
Write a resumable rebuild loop for NutritionProfile read models. Constraints: batch-size parameter, revision cursor, persist each batch, honour a `from` option for crash recovery.
Build a CQRS split in TypeScript for Recipe: handleCommand writes via decide + appendToStream, queryView reads from an eventually consistent read store. Share the event DU but not the state type.
Explain briefly why a CQRS split uses distinct state types for the write and read sides, then show a typed DietPlan implementation with handleCommand and queryView.

Build a stateless CookingSession saga that reacts to events and emits the next command: when CookingSessionStarted, emit StartTimer; when CookingSessionFinished, emit ArchiveSession. Return null for unrelated events.

Write a 3-step FoodInspection process manager. Constraints: stateless, pure react function, return null for unrelated events. Flow: InspectionScheduled -> AssignInspector, InspectorAssigned -> ReserveVehicle, ReportFiled -> NotifyFacility.
Build a translate function that maps internal Recipe domain events to integration events with a versioned schema string. Some internal events translate to null (internal-only).
What's the transactional outbox idiom for FoodInspection events wrapped in Effect.gen? Show a TypeScript appendWithOutbox that writes events and outbox rows atomically. The publisher is out of scope.
Build a typed decide function for a RecipeTest aggregate with domain guards: cannot rate a test that has not started; rating must be 1-5. Use an exhaustive switch and a typed InvariantError.

Add a typed `when` reducer and an aggregate helper around this PantryStock event DU so we can fold event histories back into aggregate state.

```typescript
type PantryStockEvent = { type: 'opened'; id: string } | { type: 'stocked'; grams: number } | { type: 'drawn'; grams: number };
```
Build an Effect.gen-wrapped projection runner for MealSwap. Read events from a feed, project them, and write the updated view to the read store, one event at a time.
